steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Two independent clauses must be joined correctly: one about the Longyearbyen observatory in Norway and one about Buckland Park in Australia. The blank determines the punctuation between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — hemisphere and: Uses a coordinating conjunction without a preceding comma — creates a run-on by fusing two independent clauses without proper punctuation. ✗
  • Choice B — hemisphere: No punctuation at all — fuses two independent clauses into a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — hemisphere,: A comma alone joining two independent clauses is a comma splice — a comma cannot substitute for a semicolon or conjunction+comma. ✗
  • Choice D — hemisphere;: A semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses without a conjunction. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
